test.name: Detect a broken foreign key in person_evidence and surface it at the top
test.description: The headline integrity feature. person_evidence entry pe_003 references person I9, which does not exist in tree.gedcomx.json (only I1 and I2 do). The skill must detect this dangling reference and surface it as a warning at the top of the report, per SKILL.md Step 2 and the 'surface warnings prominently' rule. Uses the exact pe_003 -> I9 shape from the skill's own warning example.

input.user_message: What's the current status of this research project?

judge_context: Should detect that person_evidence entry pe_003 references person 'I9', which does not exist in tree.gedcomx.json (tree contains only I1 Patrick and I2 Thomas)

The broken-foreign-key warning must be surfaced PROMINENTLY at the top of the report, not buried inside the narrative — per the SKILL.md rule 'Surface warnings prominently'. The warning should name the offending entry (pe_003) and the missing person id (I9)
Should NOT invent or assume an identity for I9, and should NOT silently ignore the dangling reference or treat the project as clean
Should still produce the rest of the status summary (the dangling reference is one warning, not a reason to abort) — read-only, both summaries still expected
Should NOT modify research.json or tree.gedcomx.json to 'fix' the reference — this skill is read-only; the right move is to flag it for the user
